LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT PATRICK TAYLOR'S MOTION TO STAY EVICTION PROCEEDING AND SET ADIDE DEFAULT; AND FOR NEW TRIAL: DENIED. MOTIONS FOR ALL OTHER FORMS OF RELIEF ARE DENIED FOR FAILURE TO JUSTIFY THE REQUEST. HOWEVER, THE COURT DETERMINES A STAY OF ONE WEEK AS TO DEFENDANTS TAYLOR (1ST STAY) AND REICHART (2ND STAY) IS APPROPRIATE. ORDERS SIGNED. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar May-25-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patrick Taylor, Defendant Propria Persona
Jeremy Heath Reichart, Defendant Propria Persona
Michael C. Hall, Esq. (415) 512-9865 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Koral
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: DEFENDANT PATRICK TAYLOR'S Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint and Request For Sanctions: MOOT as Defendant has appeared by requesting affirmative relief. Motions for all other forms of relief are DENIED for failure to justify the request. (301/JLW/) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Notice Of Motion To Stay Eviction Proceeding And Set Adide Default; And For New Trial
Off Calendar May-25-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patrick Taylor, Defendant Propria Persona
Jeremy Heath Reichart, Defendant Propria Persona
Michael C. Hall, Esq. (415) 512-9865 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Koral
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling:
DEFENDANT PATRICK TAYLOR'S Motion To Stay Eviction Proceeding And Set Adide Default; And For New Trial: DENIED. Motions for all other forms of relief are DENIED for failure to justify the request. However, the Court determines a stay of one week as to Defendants Taylor (1st stay) and Reichart (2nd stay) is appropriate. Orders signed. (301/JLW/)
